content_en:
This clear prohibition allows for no interpretation.

Question: When it is necessary to greet the People of the Book, may we greet them with greetings of peace?

Answer: The prohibition in the previous hadith is clear and is

' Related by Muslim (2055)

Chapter -2 The Manners Of Giving Greetings Of Peace 67

not open to interpretation. When it is necessary to greet them, one should use a wording other than the \slamic greeting. For example, one may say, “How are you,” or anything similar. Abu Sa‘ad said, “If one wants to greet a Dhimee (one from the People of the Book who lives in a Muslim land by agreement and under the protection of the Muslims), he should not do so with ‘AsSalaam,’ but with, ‘May Allah guide you,’ or, ‘May Allah make pleasant for you your morning.”” Commenting on Abu Sa‘ad’s words, An-Nawawee said, “What Abu Sa’‘ad said is fine for when one needs to [greet them]. One may say, ‘Good morning,’ or, ‘Happiness to your morning,’ or, ‘Good health to your morning’ ...or anything similar. But if one does not need to do so, then the chosen view is to say nothing, for in saying those greetings, we show a display of love when we are ordered not to love them...And Allah knows best.”

13) Answering A Greeting From The People Of The Book With “Wa- ‘Alaikum (And The Same Upon You)”

Ina narration related by Anas Ibn Maalik , the Messenger of Allah # said,

“When [someone from] the People of the Book gives you greetings of peace, say, ‘Wa-‘Alaikum.’”"1)

Question: When we hear someone from the People of the Book clearly and unequivocally say, ‘As-Salaamu-‘Alaikum (Peace be upon you), should we respond with, ‘Wa-‘Alaikum,’ in applying the apparent meaning of the previous hadith, or should we reply, ‘Wa-‘Alaikumus-Salaam'?

Answer: Some scholars maintain that, if we are sure that he gave greetings of peace, we should answer with, ‘Wa-‘AlaikumusSalaam.’"] They say that this is in keeping with the principles of

"I Bukhaaree (6258) and Muslim (2163) "I In another narration, the Prophet # explained that some Jews would pass by and say, ‘As-Saam ‘Alaikum,’ which sounds like, ‘As-Salaamu ‘Alaikum,

justice, and Allah 3% ordered us to be just and generous in our dealings. Others hold - and their opinion is stronger - that we should respond to their greetings with the wording that is related in the previously mentioned authentic hadith: ‘“Wa-’Alaikum (And the same upon you).””

14) When You Come Across A Gathering Which Consists Of A Mix Of Muslims And Disbelievers, It Is Permissible To Initiate Greetings Of Peace

The Prophet # was once riding on a donkey, upon which there was a saddle, and underneath the saddle there was a coarse Fadakee (ascribed to a place call Fadak) garment; and Usaamah Ibn Zaid 4 was seated behind the Prophet #%. The Prophet 2% was visiting Sa‘ad [bn ‘Ubaadah % in the precincts of Banee AlHaarith Ibn Al-Khazraj, and this was before the Battle of Badr. They passed by a gathering that contained a mix of Muslims, polytheists that worshipped idols, and Jews. ‘Abdullah Ibn Ubai Tbn Sulool - who later outwardly accepted Islam and become well known as the leader of the hypocrites - and ‘Abdullah Ibn Rawaahah were among those seated in the gathering. When the dust of the mount was raised over the gathering, ‘Abdullah Ibn Ubai covered his nose with his robe and then said, “Do not raise dust upon us.” The Messenger of Allah #% then extended to them greetings of peace, after which he % stopped, descended, and invited them unto Allah 4. And he recited the Qur’an to them..."

Scholars agree that it is permissible to initiate greetings of peace to a gathering that contains a mix of Muslims and disbelievers; An-Nawawee related this consensus. And this ruling is not in conflict with the hadith in which the Prophet #% forbade Muslims from initiating greetings of peace with the People of the

but which in Hebrew means, ‘Death upon you.’ This is why we should respond with, ‘And the same upon you.’ But if one is sure that they did indeed say, ‘As-Salaamu-‘Alaikum, then some scholars hold that one should respond with, ‘Wa-’Alaikumus-Salaam.’

5) Bukhaaree (6254) and Muslim (1798)

Chapter -2 The Manners Of Giving Greetings Of Peace 69 Book, because that hadith applies to situations wherein one encounters an individual from the People of the Book or a group of them (without any Muslims being among them). When there are both Muslims and disbelievers in a gathering, one extends greetings of peace with the intention of addressing the Muslims only. It was said to Imam Ahmad, “We have dealings with Jews and Christians, and we go to their homes. When we go to them, they [sometimes] have a group of Muslims with them, so should we give greetings of peace to [all of] then?” He said, ‘Yes, but you should intend your greetings of peace for the Muslims.” AnNawawee issued the same ruling, saying that it is Sunnah to greet them all but that one should have the intention that his greetings are only directed at the Muslim or Muslims that are present.

Question: When there is a group that contains a mix of Muslims and disbelievers, should one give this greeting: ““As-Salaamu ‘Ala Man-Ittaba’al-Huda (Peace upon he who follows the [true] guidance).”

Answer: No, one should not give this greeting but should instead give the standard Islamic greeting, with the intention of directing it to the Muslims only. Ibn Al-Uthaimeen said, ‘If there is a mixed group, containing Muslims and Christians, one should give them the customary greetings of peace, ‘Assalaamu ‘Alaikum, with the intention of addressing the Muslims among them.”

15) If One Has A Valid Islamic Excuse, He May Signal His Greeting Instead Of Uttering It

It is generally forbidden to greet people with a gesture, because it is a practice that is common among the People of the Book, and we are ordered to differ from them. At-Tirmidhee related a hadith in which it is made clear that it is forbidden to greet others with a gesture and that doing so is something that is particular to the People of the Book; however, At-Tirmidhee described the narration as containing some Gharaabah (an indication of weakness in the narration), About the same narration, Al-Haafidh Ibn Hajr said, ‘Its chain contains some weakness,”” Nonetheless, with a good chain, An-Nisaaee related

the following hadith from Jaabir 4:

“Do not greet fone another] with the greeting of the Jews; their greeting is with their heads, their hands, and with a gesture.’"!

Al-Haafidh said, “The prohibition of giving As-Salaam with a gesture is specific to those that have the ability, both practicably and according to the Shariah, to utter the greeting. If one is busy or unable to utter the greeting - for example, one is busy with prayer, one is too far away from the other person, one is a mute, or the person is deaf - then it is legislated for him to extend the greeting with a gesture instead.”

16) It ts Permissible To Greet Someone Who Is Praying, And The Latter May Respond With A Gesture (But Not With An Utterance Of The Greeting)

Although many do not know this, it is in fact permissible to give

"1 Related by At-Tirmidhee (2697), and the wording here is from his narration. Ahmad (27014), Ibn Maajah (3701), Ad-Daarimee (2637), and Al-Bukhaaree (in Al-Adab Al-Mufrad - 1003, 1047) - each of these related it as well. And in his grading of the hadith, Al-Albaanee said, “Sahech (authentic).”

1 Abu Daawood (5204)

Chapter -2 The Manners Of Giving Greetings Of Peace 71

‘As-Salaam’ to someone who is praying. The Prophet’s Companions 4 would greet him while he was praying, and he would neither reproach nor blame them for doing so. His silent approval proves that it is permissible to greet someone while he is praying. Once Jaabir 4 came across the Prophet #% while he was praying. Jaabir 4 gave him greetings of peace, and the Prophet #2 responded with a gesture. After he completed his prayer, the Prophet 3% called Jaabir 2 and said,

“You gave me greetings of peace earlier, and I was praying.”

In another narration, Suhaib said, “I passed by the Messenger of Allah # while he was praying, and I gave him ‘As-Salaam,’ and he returned the greeting with a gesture.” One of the narrators said, ‘From what I know, he said, ‘He gestured with his finger.’”"] These and other narrations prove that it is permissible to greet someone who is praying and that he may respond with a gesture.

Question: When one is praying and responds to a greeting with a gesture, what form should that gesture take?

Answer: There is no specified way of gesturing a response to a greeting during prayer. This is because various narrations describe different ways in which the Prophet # signaled his response; sometimes it was with his finger (as in the abovementioned hadith of Suhaib 4); once it was with his hand, as is related in the hadith of Jaabir ; and once with a nod of his head, as in the narration of Ibn Mas’ood 4. The author of ‘Aoun Al-Ma’bood said, “From these different narrations, we gather that the Prophet # gestured one way on a certain occasion, and another way ona different occasion. Therefore, all of those forms of gesturing are permissible. And Allah 3 knows best.”

1) Related by Muslim (540) @ Related by Abu Daawood (925), and Al-Albaanee said, “sahih,” in sahih Abu Daawood (818)

17) It Is Permissible To Greet Someone Who Is Reciting The Quran, And If One Extends The Greeting, The Reciter Must Respond

Some scholars have ruled that it is permissible to greet someone who is busy reciting the Qur’an, while others have ruled that it is forbidden to do so. The former group of scholars is correct, for there is no proof to show that the reciter is an exception to many revealed texts that exhort Muslims to spread greetings of peace to one another and that command Muslims to responds to those greetings. True, a reciter is occupied with the highest form of remembrance; but that does not prevent someone from greeting him, nor is it less than compulsory for him to respond.
content_id:
Larangan yang jelas ini tidak dapat ditafsirkan lain.

Pertanyaan: Ketika diperlukan untuk menyapa Ahli Kitab, bolehkah kita menyapa mereka dengan salam Islam?

Jawaban: Larangan dalam hadis sebelumnya jelas dan tidak terbuka untuk penafsiran lain. Ketika memang diperlukan untuk menyapa mereka, gunakanlah redaksi selain salam Islam. Misalnya, seseorang dapat mengatakan, "Apa kabar," atau hal yang serupa. Abu Sa'ad berkata: "Jika seseorang ingin menyapa seorang Dzimmi (orang dari Ahli Kitab yang tinggal di negeri Muslim berdasarkan perjanjian dan di bawah perlindungan kaum Muslim), ia tidak boleh melakukannya dengan 'As-Salam,' tetapi dengan 'Semoga Allah memberimu petunjuk,' atau 'Semoga Allah membuat paginya menyenangkan bagimu.'" Al-Nawawi berkomentar: "Apa yang dikatakan Abu Sa'ad itu baik untuk kondisi ketika seseorang perlu (menyapa mereka)."

13) Membalas Salam dari Ahli Kitab dengan "Wa-'Alaikum (Dan Begitu Juga Atasmu)"

Dalam riwayat Anas Ibn Malik, Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: "Ketika (seseorang dari) Ahli Kitab memberi salam kepadamu, ucapkanlah: 'Wa-'Alaikum.'"

Para ulama yang berpendapat lebih kuat mengatakan bahwa kita harus menjawab salam mereka dengan redaksi yang terdapat dalam hadis sahih yang telah disebutkan: "Wa-'Alaikum (Dan begitu juga atasmu)."

14) Ketika Bertemu Kelompok Campuran Muslim dan Non-Muslim, Dibolehkan Memulai Salam

Nabi ﷺ pernah berkendara dengan keledai dan melewati sebuah perkumpulan yang terdiri dari campuran kaum Muslim, kaum musyrik penyembah berhala, dan orang-orang Yahudi. Nabi ﷺ mengucapkan salam kepada mereka setelah itu beliau berhenti, turun, dan mengajak mereka kepada Allah. Dan beliau membacakan Al-Quran kepada mereka.

Para ulama sepakat bahwa dibolehkan untuk memulai salam kepada perkumpulan yang berisi campuran kaum Muslim dan non-Muslim; Al-Nawawi menyebutkan ijma ini. Dan hukum ini tidak bertentangan dengan hadis di mana Nabi ﷺ melarang kaum Muslim memulai salam kepada Ahli Kitab, karena hadis tersebut berlaku untuk situasi di mana seseorang bertemu individu dari Ahli Kitab atau sekelompok mereka (tanpa ada kaum Muslim di antara mereka). Ketika ada Muslim dan non-Muslim dalam sebuah perkumpulan, seseorang mengucapkan salam dengan niat hanya menyapa kaum Muslim.

Pertanyaan: Ketika ada kelompok campuran yang berisi kaum Muslim dan non-Muslim, haruskah seseorang mengucapkan salam ini: "As-Salamu 'Ala Man-Ittaba'al-Huda (Semoga keselamatan atas orang yang mengikuti petunjuk)?"

Jawaban: Tidak, seseorang tidak boleh mengucapkan salam ini, tetapi hendaknya menggunakan salam Islam yang standar, dengan niat hanya ditujukan kepada kaum Muslim.

15) Jika Ada Uzur Syar'i, Seseorang Boleh Memberi Isyarat sebagai Pengganti Salam Lisan

Secara umum dilarang menyapa orang dengan isyarat, karena itu adalah kebiasaan Ahli Kitab dan kita diperintahkan untuk berbeda dari mereka. Al-Hafidz berkata: "Larangan memberi salam dengan isyarat khusus bagi mereka yang mampu, baik secara praktis maupun menurut Syariat, untuk mengucapkan salam. Jika seseorang sibuk atau tidak mampu mengucapkan salam — misalnya, sedang salat, terlalu jauh dari orang lain, bisu, atau orang yang disapa tuli — maka disunahkan baginya untuk menyampaikan salam dengan isyarat."

16) Dibolehkan Memberi Salam kepada Orang yang Sedang Salat, dan yang Salat Boleh Membalas dengan Isyarat

Meskipun banyak yang tidak mengetahuinya, sesungguhnya dibolehkan untuk memberi salam kepada orang yang sedang salat. Para sahabat Nabi ﷺ biasa memberi salam kepada beliau ketika beliau sedang salat, dan beliau tidak menegur atau menyalahkan mereka. Suatu ketika Jabir bertemu Nabi ﷺ saat beliau sedang salat. Jabir memberi salam, dan Nabi ﷺ membalasnya dengan isyarat. Setelah menyelesaikan salatnya, Nabi ﷺ memanggil Jabir dan berkata: "Kamu tadi memberi salam kepadaku, dan aku sedang salat."

Pertanyaan: Ketika seseorang sedang salat dan membalas salam dengan isyarat, bentuk isyarat seperti apa yang harus ia lakukan?

Jawaban: Tidak ada cara yang ditentukan untuk memberi isyarat membalas salam dalam salat. Ini karena berbagai riwayat menggambarkan cara yang berbeda-beda ketika Nabi ﷺ memberi isyarat balasan; terkadang dengan jarinya, terkadang dengan tangannya, dan terkadang dengan anggukan kepalanya. Oleh karena itu, semua bentuk isyarat tersebut diperbolehkan.

17) Dibolehkan Memberi Salam kepada Orang yang Sedang Membaca Al-Quran, dan yang Membaca Wajib Membalas

Sebagian ulama berpendapat bahwa dibolehkan memberi salam kepada orang yang sedang sibuk membaca Al-Quran, sementara sebagian lain berpendapat bahwa itu dilarang. Kelompok pertama lebih tepat, karena tidak ada dalil yang menunjukkan bahwa pembaca Al-Quran dikecualikan dari banyak teks wahyu yang memerintahkan kaum Muslim untuk menyebarkan salam dan wajib membalasnya.
content_ar:
وهذا النهي الصريح لا يحتمل التأويل.

سؤال: إذا اضطر المسلم إلى تحية أهل الكتاب، فهل يجوز أن يُحييهم بتحية السلام الإسلامية؟

الجواب: النهي في الحديث المتقدم صريح لا يقبل التأويل. فإن دعت الضرورة إلى التحية، استُعمل لفظٌ آخر غير تحية الإسلام الخاصة. فيجوز للمسلم في هذه الحال أن يقول: «أهلًا» أو «صباح الخير» أو ما شابه ذلك من العبارات المعتادة. ومناط الحكم أن يحتفظ بالسلام الإسلامي شعارًا خاصًّا بالمسلمين لا يُفيض به على غيرهم.

أخرجه مسلم (2055).

الفصل الثاني: آداب إلقاء السلام.

وفيما يتعلق بالردّ على سلام أهل الكتاب: فقد أجاز النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم الردّ بـ«وعليكم» اقتصارًا. والمقصود بهذا الردّ المختصر إقرار الخير لهم من غير التزام بلفظ الدعاء الكامل الخاص بالمسلمين، وهذا موقف متوازن يُراعي الواقع الاجتماعي دون المساس بخصوصية شعائر الإسلام. وقد أصدرت اللجنة الدائمة للبحوث العلمية والإفتاء فتاوى عدة في هذا الباب تتسق مع هذا الفهم.
